lede: A look at November 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jeremy Swayman and Joseba Llorente, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Hibiscus Rising
events.desc: Hibiscus Rising, commemorating David Oluwale, is unveiled in Leeds.

events.title: 2022 Malaysian general election
events.desc: Five days after the general elections which resulted in a hung parliament, opposition leader and former deputy prime minister Anwar Ibrahim is officially named as the 10th prime minister of Malaysia.

events.title: Avdhela Project
events.desc: The Avdhela Project, an Aromanian digital library and cultural initiative, is founded in Bucharest, Romania.

events.title: Velvet Revolution
events.desc: After a week of mass protests against the Communist regime known as the Velvet Revolution, Miloš Jakeš and the entire Politburo of the Czechoslovak Communist Party resign from office. This brings an effective end to Communist rule in Czechoslovakia.

events.title: 1976 Çaldıran–Muradiye earthquake
events.desc: The Çaldıran–Muradiye earthquake in eastern Turkey kills between 4,000 and 5,000 people.

events.title: Battle of Makin
events.desc: World War II: At the battle of Makin the USS Liscome Bay is torpedoed near Tarawa and sinks, killing 650 men.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The First Slovak Republic becomes a signatory to the Tripartite Pact, officially joining the Axis powers.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Lookout Mountain: Near Chattanooga, Tennessee, Union forces under General Ulysses S. Grant capture Lookout Mountain and begin to break the Confederate siege of the city led by General Braxton Bragg.

events.title: Charles Darwin
events.desc: British naturalist Charles Darwin's On the Origin of Species is published.

events.title: South Carolina
events.desc: South Carolina passes the Ordinance of Nullification, declaring that the Tariffs of 1828 and 1832 were null and void in the state, beginning the Nullification Crisis.

deaths.text: Jimmy Cliff, Jamaican Hall of Fame reggae singer-songwriter
Matthew Bucksbaum, American businessman and philanthropist, co-founded General Growth Properties
Anna Jarvis, American founder of Mother's Day

observances.text: Christian feast days: Andrew Dũng-Lạc, Pierre Dumoulin-Borie, and other Vietnamese Martyrs
